ref: and said, “Please come to my home where I can serve you.
text: na ɔhane kyɛ, “Me Mene, sɛ alo me anwu me anwumvoin de a, nzaade mesrɛ wɔ, nnesã w’akoaa nwo kɔ.
translated: And he said, "My Lord, if it is my time to die, then I am ready, but I beg of you, have mercy on my child."

ref: Then he put the branches where the sheep and goats would see them while they were drinking from the water trough. The goats mated there
text: Ɔfale mmakaa bɔ wawɔngyewɔngye bɛ nwo ne guale nningyein bɔ ngaka nemɔ no nu nzue ne anwo. Né sɛ ngaka nemɔ ba kyɛ bɛbaano nzue a, berɛ yeɛ bɛfo bɛ nwo ɔ.
translated: The hunter's dog is barking loudly in the forest, and the hunter is shouting at the top of his voice, but the animal has escaped into the bush. If the hunter and his dog had been quiet, perhaps they would have caught the animal.

ref: Jacob said, “Now tell me your name.” “Don't you know who I am?” he asked. And he blessed Jacob.
text: Yeɛ Yakob sele ye kyɛ, “Mesrɛ wɔ, bɔ wɔ dumaa kyire me.” Na Biaa ne buale kyɛ, “Nzu ati yeɛ ebisa me dumaa ɔ?” Na oyirale Yakob wɔ berɛ.
translated: Yacob said to his wife, "My sister, come and let us run away." And the woman asked, "Where will we run to?" And Yacob's wife refused.

ref: So God created humans to be like himself; he made men and women.
